title: Jesus, the name high over all (revised)
firstLine: Jesus, the name high over all,
publicDomainBasis: Charles Wesley (1707–1788), Carl Gottholf Glaser (1784–1829). All public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.text:
Jesus, the name high over all,
In hell, or earth, or sky:
Angels and men before it fall,
And devils fear and fly.

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.text:
Jesus, the name to sinners dear,
The name to sinners giv’n.
It scatters all their guilty fear;
It brings them peace of heav’n.

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.text:
Jesus the prisoner’s fetters breaks
And bruises Satan’s head;
Pow’r into strengthless souls He speaks,
And life into the dead.

sections.label: Verse 4
sections.text:
Oh, that the world might taste and see
The riches of His grace!
The arms of love that compass me,
Would all mankind embrace.

sections.label: Verse 5
sections.text:
The name of Jesus, ever sure,
Sufficient, life to bear,
So dear, so precious, shall endure,
My testimony e’er.
